theme: Cultural Bridge - Bridging Traditions and Modern Life

emotionalArc.struggle: Parents' expectations for 'safe' careers (doctor, lawyer, engineer) vs. child's desire for creative/passion careers. Pressure to take over family business vs. pursuing own dreams. Guilt over 'wasting' parents' sacrifices.
emotionalArc.crisis: Locking self in bathroom and crying. 'It's not too late to become a doctor' - burst into tears. 'I didn't think you'd be so ordinary' - father's words. 'I studied communications and my dad said I would end up homeless.'
emotionalArc.perseverance: Changed major just days before freshman year. Switched majors without telling parents until third year. Quit law school to pursue event planning. Moved away to 'fully be yourself.'
emotionalArc.breakthrough: 'Maybe choosing my own dreams is the ultimate expression of my gratitude.' 'I'm so proud of you — I'd never heard her say that in my entire life.' Finding balance between honoring immigrant identity AND personal dreams.

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Locking self in bathroom and crying while working at father's restaurant (teenage years)
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Feeling trapped, suffocated by expectations, internal conflict

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'It's not too late to become a doctor' - mother says seriously after master's degree, burst into tears
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Parental expectations never ending, achievements not recognized, emotional breakdown

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'I didn't think you'd be so ordinary' - father's words to daughter
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Parental disappointment, feeling like failure, harsh judgment

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'I studied communications and my dad said I would end up homeless'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Parental fear, rejection of non-traditional path, harsh reality

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Graduated third in high school class, mom disappointed (not salutatorian or valedictorian)
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Never good enough, impossible standards, constant pressure

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Changed major just days before freshman year (architecture to sociology/psychology)
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Courage to follow heart, last-minute decision, breaking expectations

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Switched majors without telling parents until third year (pre-med to marketing)
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Hiding truth, fear of disappointment, secret pursuit of passion

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Quit law school after first semester ('I'm miserable')
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Courage to quit 'safe' path, following passion, risking stability

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Translating for father at back-to-school night in 'mediocre Spanish'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Role reversal, language barriers, child as translator

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'It still breaks my heart knowing he won't understand what I went through'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Emotional distance, language barrier creating disconnect, sadness

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Called 'white-washed' with 'gringa accent' by family friends
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Not belonging to either culture, identity rejection, isolation

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'I had no idea what people actually did in an office'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Lack of exposure, limited career knowledge, first-gen challenges

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'I'm so proud of you — I'd never heard her say that in my entire life'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Breakthrough moment, parental acceptance, validation after years

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'There's constant guilt of "you have these luxuries that we were never given"'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Immigrant child guilt, pressure to succeed, unspoken expectations

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'Am I really supposed to follow in their footsteps when everything they did was to make sure I could make choices for myself?'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Realization, paradox of immigrant dreams, choosing own path

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'Maybe choosing my own dreams is the ultimate expression of my gratitude'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Reframing gratitude, honoring sacrifices through independence, breakthrough

commonThemes: Career trinity pressure: doctor, engineer, lawyer as only acceptable paths
Parents' credentials not recognized in new country → projected expectations onto children
Guilt over 'wasting' parents' sacrifices by choosing different path
Lack of exposure to creative careers ('I had no idea what people actually did in an office')
Language barriers creating role reversal (children translating for parents)
Not fully belonging to either culture ('white-washed,' 'gringa accent')
Cultural traditions that don't match American media depictions
Collectivist culture (family honor, inherited paths) vs. individualist culture (personal choice)
Parents' survival mindset vs. children's growth mindset
eslResonanceMultipliers: Communication & Language Barriers (translating for parents, language disconnect)
Belonging & Identity (stuck between two worlds, not fully belonging anywhere)
Overcoming 'Not Good Enough' (never good enough, impossible standards)
Building New Life (parents' immigration journey, children creating new path)
Connection Across Differences (finding others who share experience)
Persistence Despite Setbacks (changing majors, quitting law school, pursuing passion)

characterProfile.genericName: To be chosen from available names (avoid Mei from cultural-bridge-1, consider: Chen, Jin, Li, Wei, Raj, Yuki, Sakura from Asian names)
characterProfile.culturalBackground: Composite from 3 sources (Greek-American, various Asian-American, Salvadoran-American backgrounds)
characterProfile.ageRange: Story spans teenage years through young adulthood (college age)
characterProfile.familyDynamics: Parents immigrated for better life, want child to succeed in 'safe' careers, don't understand creative paths
characterProfile.internalConflict: Caught between honoring parents' sacrifices and following own dreams
characterProfile.externalConflict: Parental pressure for traditional careers vs. personal passion, language barriers, cultural disconnect
characterProfile.transformation: Pressure and guilt → courage to choose own path → realization that choosing own dreams honors parents' sacrifices
